intro: The clearest gap in the data is developer experience: Lucide sits at 5/5, Feather Icons at 4/5. Teams feel that gap day-to-day. Lucide is built for modern web apps wanting a clean, consistent, community-maintained icon set and is not a fit for projects needing multiple visual weights, duotone styles, or mobile-native icon packs. Feather Icons is built for legacy projects already using feather or fans of the minimalist single-weight style and is not a fit for new projects needing active updates, more icons, or multiple weights. On the scoring side, Lucide rates 5/5 for developer experience and 5/5 for transparency; Feather Icons rates 4/5 and 5/5 respectively. The honest trade-off: with Lucide you accept that single visual weight limits stylistic variation, while Feather Icons comes with the reality that maintenance has been slow for years. Neither side is free. Lucide's headline strength in the data is that actively maintained fork of feather with 1000+ icons.
quick_take: Lucide is for modern web apps wanting a clean, consistent, community-maintained icon set; Feather Icons is for legacy projects already using feather or fans of the minimalist single-weight style; decide.
verdict_a: Choose Lucide if you are modern web apps wanting a clean, consistent, community-maintained icon set and developer ergonomics (5/5) matter more than niche fit.
verdict_b: Choose Feather Icons if you are legacy projects already using feather or fans of the minimalist single-weight style and its workflow specificity outweighs a 4/5 DX rating.

faq.q: Is Lucide cheaper than Feather Icons?
faq.a: Both list a free pricing model in our data, and both publish a free tier. We don't have per-seat numbers in this dataset, so 'cheaper' depends on volume. Price both on your actual usage before deciding.

faq.q: Can I migrate from Lucide to Feather Icons?
faq.a: Lucide has low lock-in (oss icon library); Feather Icons has low lock-in (oss svg icon set). Migration effort scales with how much state and automation you've built in Lucide; plan for data export, config rebuild, and a parallel-run period before cutting over.

In our data, Lucide scores 5/5 for developer experience versus 4/5 for Feather Icons. That said, DX is subjective; the Feather Icons score may still be acceptable if its feature fit is stronger for your use case.

faq.q: Is Feather Icons a good alternative to Lucide?
faq.a: Feather Icons is built for legacy projects already using feather or fans of the minimalist single-weight style and explicitly not for new projects needing active updates, more icons, or multiple weights, so it's a fit only if your workflow matches its stated audience. If you were using Lucide for modern web apps wanting a clean, consistent, community-maintained icon set, the switch is reasonable; outside that scope, look elsewhere.
